reasoning: `leads_to_unfold` is external_body bridging `leads_to`'s definition — `always(p.implies(eventually(q)))` with its nested `TempPred::new` closures — to a bare forall in the ensures. This is an unverified trust assumption resolving the intensional closure gap.

`implies_apply` is external_body bridging `p.implies(q).satisfied_by(ex)` — which evaluates a fresh closure from `TempPred::new` — to the direct modus ponens conclusion. This is a separate unverified trust assumption resolving the intensional closure gap.

`execution_equality` forces intensional `spec_fn` equality from pointwise extensional equivalence. This is the root unverified trust assumption — structurally distinct closures are collapsed to equal without proof.

`eventually_proved_by_witness` is external_body introducing a concrete witness into `eventually`'s `TempPred::new` existential closure. This bridges the intensional gap between a known `p.satisfied_by(ex.suffix(witness_idx))` and the closure-wrapped exists — a separate unverified trust assumption.
An unsatisfiable spec vacuously entailing anything is standard ex falso quodlibet. This is not a spec gap.
